title: Pilosella tricholepia
taxonomy: Pilosella tricholepia is classified within the family Asteraceae and the genus Pilosella. The genus Pilosella belongs to the tribe Cichorieae, and the family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This classification places Pilosella tricholepia within the larger clade of daisy and sunflower plants. Pilosella tricholepia is a member of the subtribe Hieraciinae, which includes several other genera of plants with similar characteristics. The exact taxonomic classification may be subject to revision as new research becomes available.

intro: Pilosella tricholepis, also known as Alpine Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella tricholepis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Pilosella tricholepis include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are often used to treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
